summary: Asherman syndrome commonly causes menstrual abnormalities such as amenorrhea or hypomenorrhea due to damage leading to impaired endometrial regrowth. Hypomenorrhea specifically reflects reduced but not absent uterine lining development resulting from scarring within the uterus.
formatted_summary: agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Asherman syndrome commonly causes menstrual abnormalities such as amenorrhea or hypomenorrhea due to damage leading to impaired endometrial regrowth. Hypomenorrhea specifically reflects reduced but not absent uterine lining development resulting from scarring within the uterus.. Answer choice: B
